common.binary

Class AbstractBinaryStream

  • java.lang.Object
    • common.binary.AbstractBinaryStream
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    AbstractRandomAccessStream, ByteArrayReader, ByteArrayReaderOld

    public abstract class AbstractBinaryStream
extends java.lang.Object
    Base class for binary input and output streams that provides common position management, byte-order handling, and mark/reset support.

    Subclasses are responsible for implementing the actual I/O operations, while this class supplies shared navigation and stream-state behaviour.

    The following diagram shows the class hierarchy. 
      
 <interface> AutoCloseable
 ├──  <interface> BinaryInput
 └──  <interface> BinaryOutput

 AbstractBinaryStream (Root Base)
 ├── ByteArrayReader [implements BinaryInput]
 └── AbstractRandomAccessStream [implements AutoCloseable]
     ├── RandomAccessReader [implements BinaryInput]
     └── RandomAccessWriter [implements BinaryOutput]
    Since:
    9 June 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      protected java.nio.ByteOrder byteOrder  
      protected java.util.Deque<java.lang.Long> positionStack

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      protected void checkBounds(long byteCount)
      Verifies that the specified number of bytes remain available.
      java.nio.ByteOrder getByteOrder()
      Returns the current byte order used for interpreting data.
      abstract long getCurrentPosition()  
      boolean hasRemaining()
      Determines whether at least one byte remains in the stream.
      boolean hasRemaining(int n)
      Determines whether at least the specified number of bytes remain in the stream.
      abstract long length()  
      void mark()
      Saves the current stream position on the internal mark stack.
      long remaining()
      Returns the number of bytes remaining between the current position and the end of the stream.
      void reset()
      Restores the stream position registered by the most recent mark().
      abstract void seek(long position)  
      void setByteOrder(java.nio.ByteOrder order)
      Sets the byte order used when reading or writing multi-byte values.
      void skip(long n)
      Moves the current stream position by the specified offset.

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • positionStack

        protected final java.util.Deque<java.lang.Long> positionStack

      • byteOrder

        protected java.nio.ByteOrder byteOrder

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractBinaryStream

        protected AbstractBinaryStream(java.nio.ByteOrder order)
        Constructs a binary stream with the specified byte order.
        Parameters:
        order - the byte order to use
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if order is null

    • Method Detail

      • length

        public abstract long length()
                     throws java.io.IOException
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • getCurrentPosition

        public abstract long getCurrentPosition()
                                 throws java.io.IOException
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • seek

        public abstract void seek(long position)
                   throws java.io.IOException
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • skip

        public void skip(long n)
          throws java.io.IOException
        Moves the current stream position by the specified offset.
        Parameters:
        n - the number of bytes to move. Positive values move forward and negative values move backward
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the resulting position is outside the stream bounds
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • mark

        public void mark()
        Saves the current stream position on the internal mark stack.

        A subsequent call to reset() restores the most recently saved position.

        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if the stream position cannot be queried due to an underlying I/O error

      • reset

        public void reset()
        Restores the stream position registered by the most recent mark().

        The restored position is removed from the mark stack.

        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if no marked position exists
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if the stream position cannot be restored due to an underlying I/O error

      • setByteOrder

        public void setByteOrder(java.nio.ByteOrder order)
        Sets the byte order used when reading or writing multi-byte values.
        Parameters:
        order - the byte order to use
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if order is null

      • getByteOrder

        public java.nio.ByteOrder getByteOrder()
        Returns the current byte order used for interpreting data.
        Returns:
        the active ByteOrder, either ByteOrder.BIG_ENDIAN or ByteOrder.LITTLE_ENDIAN

      • remaining

        public long remaining()
        Returns the number of bytes remaining between the current position and the end of the stream.
        Returns:
        the non-negative number of remaining bytes or zero if the current position is beyond the end of the stream
        Throws:
        java.io.UncheckedIOException - if an I/O error occurs querying the stream capacity

      • hasRemaining

        public boolean hasRemaining()
        Determines whether at least one byte remains in the stream.
        Returns:
        true if at least one byte remains, otherwise false

      • hasRemaining

        public boolean hasRemaining(int n)
        Determines whether at least the specified number of bytes remain in the stream.
        Parameters:
        n - the number of bytes required
        Returns:
        true if at least n bytes remain, otherwise false
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if n is negative

      • checkBounds

        protected void checkBounds(long byteCount)
        Verifies that the specified number of bytes remain available.
        Parameters:
        byteCount - the number of bytes required
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if byteCount is negative
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if insufficient bytes remain
